hero.description: Flow is a free, offline, privacy-first expense tracker. You control your data — always.

foss.title: Free and open-source
foss.description: Flow is a GPL v3.0 licensed, free, and open-source project.

privacy.title: Privacy-focused
privacy.description_2: , and you control where your data goes

automation.title: Automated tracking
automation.description: Parse receipts with {#link href=|https://eny.gege.mn|}Eny{/link}, or automate with {#link href=|/guide/ios-shortcuts|}iOS Shortcuts{/link}.

icloud.title: iCloud backup
icloud.description: Periodically back up your data to iCloud for safe-keeping.

ux.title: Seamless UX
ux.description: Flow is built to be as user-friendly as possible, with a clean and simple UI.

currency.title: Multi-currency
currency.description: Flow supports multiple currencies and can convert between them.

footnotes.fna_1: Flow may need internet to fetch exchange rates if you use more than one currency.
footnotes.fna_2: If you use older Android devices, or devices with Windows or Linux-based operating systems, Flow may store your data and backups in an easily accessible place. Make sure your device supports containerized storage or other external access protection, as you are responsible for your financial data.

donate.title: Donate — Flow
donate.label: Support the project
donate.heading: Donate to Flow
donate.description: Flow is completely free and will always stay that way. As Flow doesn't generate any revenue, your support means the world and helps keep the project alive.

getting_started.title: Getting Started
getting_started.description: Download Flow, set up your accounts, and record your first transaction.

ios_shortcuts.title: iOS Shortcuts
ios_shortcuts.description: Automatically record expenses whenever you pay using Apple Pay.

steps.1: Open Shortcuts app, and head to Automation tab
steps.2: Tap the + button, and choose "Wallet" as a trigger
steps.3: Add/remove the cards, and categories to be used if necessary
steps.4: (Recommended) Scroll down and choose "Run Immediately". You can toggle "Notify When Run" if you want to receive a notification every time the automation runs.
steps.5: Tap Next, and choose "Create New Shortcut"
steps.6: Find "Flow" from the list of apps, and choose "Record an Expense" action
steps.7: Tap on the "Amount" field, select variable "Shortcut Input" as the source. Make sure to select the "Amount" field from the transaction fields.
steps.8: (Optional) If you're using version older than 0.19.2, put placeholders in the empty fields to avoid having to fill them in every time. You can simply put a dash (-) as a placeholder.
steps.9: (Optional) Tap on the "Account" field, and type in the account. It must be an exact match. It will fallback to your default account if left empty, or if the account name doesn't match any of your accounts.
steps.10: Tap Next, review your automation, and tap Done to save it

guide.app_version_notice: This guide is up to date with Flow {#b}{version}{/b}. Make sure you have the this version, or later installed.
guide.title: Flow Guide

what_is_flow.a: Flow is a free and open-source personal finance tracking app. As Flow is offline, you are in control of your data.

can_export.q: Can I export all of my data?
can_export.a: Yes, you can export JSON/ZIP backups which you can import to another device, and CSV files for analyzing your data in spreadsheet software (i.e., Excel, Google Sheets), which is not recoverable like JSON/ZIP backups.

import_erased.q: Importing erased my data, how to recover?
import_erased.a_before: Flow does a precaution backup before starting to import. You can find the backup in
import_erased.note_1: Please note that the precaution isn't guaranteed to succeed
import_erased.note_2: Also iOS clears the temporary storage periodically, so you may find the backup file deleted if too much time has passed.

auto_backup.a: Yes. If you're using Flow on iOS, you can also enable iCloud to save your backups. If you're using other OS, you're advised to download your manual and auto backups and save them elsewhere.

icloud_not_working.step_1: Ensure you've logged into your Apple Account on your device (in System Settings, not on Flow)
icloud_not_working.step_2: Ensure you've enabled iCloud Drive sync in System Settings > Apple Account > iCloud > iCloud Drive > Sync this iPhone (or your device's name)
icloud_not_working.step_3: Ensure you've allowed Flow to access iCloud in System Settings > Apple Account > iCloud > iCloud Drive > Apps Syncing to iCloud Drive
icloud_not_working.step_4: Flow refuses to upload the backup if it's older than the current file on iCloud. You may want to check your device's date and time settings.

works_offline.a: Yes, but there are some occurances where Flow might use an internet connection:
works_offline.item_1: Downloading exchange rates information (only if you use more than one currencies)
works_offline.item_2: Clicking on buttons that lead to external internet resources (Discord, GitHub, etc.)

multi_currency.a_1: Yes. Flow can generate reports and summaries involving accounts with different currency.
multi_currency.a_2: Reports are always displayed in your primary currency.
multi_currency.a_note: Cross-currency transfers are available on version 0.13.0 and later

currency_transfers.q: Can I make transfers between different currency accounts?
currency_transfers.a: Yes. Requires Flow version 0.13.0 or later

import_from_other.q: How to import data from other apps?
import_from_other.ivy_detail: Export a CSV backup from Ivy Wallet, and choose "Ivy Wallet" option in the import menu.
import_from_other.other_step_1: Export your data as CSV, or any other Google Sheets/Excel compatible format
import_from_other.other_step_2_prefix: Format your file to match Flow's CSV format.
import_from_other.other_step_2_note: Make sure you download the "Template" sheet

is_free.a: Yes, Flow is absolutely free. All features are available without any limitations. Please consider supporting if you can, as I make Flow on the side and it doesn't generate any revenue ^^

max_accounts.q: How many accounts can I have at max?
max_accounts.a: You can have as many accounts as you want.

feature_request.q: I don't find X features, will it be implemented?
feature_request.a_before: If you have ideas to make Flow better, please submit to our GitHub
feature_request.a_after: . As long as it aligns with Flow's vision, it will be implemented.

why_free.q: Why is Flow free and/or open-source?
why_free.a: Many benefit from open source, including me. This is my small contribution. Please consider supporting if you can, as I make Flow on the side and it doesn't generate any revenue ^^

recurring.a: Recurring transactions are available on Flow 0.14.0 or later.

how_recurring.q: How does the recurring transactions work?

icloud_how.a_1: TL;DR — Flow saves to iCloud, and the operating system uploads the file. This process cannot be sped up or altered.
icloud_how.a_2: Enabling iCloud sync on Flow will not immediately upload the data to iCloud. The operating system decides when it gets uploaded, influenced by many factors including network connection, battery level, and time of day. Refer to Apple's documentation for accurate information.
icloud_how.a_3: Interestingly, you don't need an internet connection for Flow to save to iCloud.
